session_hint: the modal not rendering above the navbar
question: What did we conclude was causing the modal to appear behind the fixed navbar?
turns: Our React modal with z-index: 9999 renders behind the fixed top navbar (z-index: 100). The backdrop covers the page body fine, but the navbar floats on top of everything including the modal.
The modal mounts inside a wrapper div that has a CSS animation applied using transform: translateY(-10px). I recall that CSS transforms can affect stacking.

Root cause: the parent container's transform property establishes a new CSS stacking context. Even though the modal has z-index: 9999, it is stacked relative only to siblings within that local context — not against the fixed navbar which lives outside it. Fix: render the modal through a React portal targeting document.body so it escapes the transformed ancestor and its z-index competes at the root stacking context.
